2605.02086.title: GETA-3DGS: Automatic Joint Structured Pruning and Quantization for 3D Gaussian Splatting
2605.02086.paper_summary: 3D Gaussian splatting (3DGS) is a state-of-the-art representation for real-time photorealistic novel-view synthesis, yet a single high-fidelity scene typically occupies hundreds of megabytes to several gigabytes, exceeding the budgets of mobile, immersive, and volumetric video platforms. Existing 3DGS compression methods (e.g., HAC++, FlexGaussian, LP-3DGS) treat pruning, quantization, and entropy coding as separate stages and rely on hand-tuned heuristics (opacity thresholds, fixed bit-widths, SH truncation), limiting cross-scene generalization and preventing users from specifying a target rate or quality budget. We propose GETA-3DGS, to our knowledge the first end-to-end automatic joint structured pruning and quantization framework for 3DGS. Building on GETA for joint pruning-quantization of deep networks, we contribute: (i) a 3DGS-aware quantization-aware dependency graph (QADG) treating each Gaussian primitive as a group with five attribute sub-nodes and degree-aware SH sub-nodes; (ii) a render-aware saliency fusing transmittance-weighted contribution, screen-space gradient, and pixel coverage into a Gaussian-level importance score; and (iii) a heterogeneous per-attribute mixed-precision scheme co-optimized with structural sparsity under a projected partial saliency-guided (PPSG) descent guarantee. On Mip-NeRF 360, Tanks and Temples, and Deep Blending, GETA-3DGS operates directly on raw Gaussian primitives rather than a post-hoc anchor representation, delivering ~5x storage reduction over Vanilla 3DGS with no per-scene thresholds. Bit-width policy is the dominant rate-distortion lever: a uniform 6-bit cap costs up to -6.74 dB on view-dependent scenes versus our heterogeneous allocation, matching an information-theoretic reverse-water-filling analysis we develop. GETA-3DGS is complementary to existing codecs: entropy coding (HAC++, CompGS) is downstream, so the two can be composed.
2605.02086.paper_summary_zh: 3D Gaussian splatting (3DGS) 是一種最先進的實時寫實新視角合成表示，然而單個高保真場景通常佔用數百MB到幾GB，超過了移動、沉浸式和體積視頻平台的預算。現有的 3DGS 壓縮方法（例如 HAC++、FlexGaussian、LP-3DGS）將修剪、量化和熵編碼視為獨立階段，並依賴手動調整的啟發式方法（不透明度閾值、固定位寬、SH 截斷），限制了跨場景的泛化，並防止用戶指定目標速率或質量預算。我們提出了 GETA-3DGS，據我們所知，這是首個端到端自動聯合結構修剪和量化框架，用於 3DGS。在 GETA 的基礎上進行深度網絡的聯合修剪-量化，我們貢獻了： (i) 一個 3DGS 感知的量化感知依賴圖（QADG），將每個高斯原語視為一組，具有五個屬性子節點和度感知的 SH 子節點； (ii) 一個渲染感知的顯著性融合，將透過加權的貢獻、屏幕空間梯度和像素覆蓋融合為高斯級別的重要性分數；以及 (iii) 一個異質的每屬性混合精度方案，與在預測的部分顯著性引導（PPSG）下降保證下共同優化的結構稀疏性。 在 Mip-NeRF 360、Tanks and Temples 和 Deep Blending 上，GETA-3DGS 直接在原始高斯原語上運行，而不是後期的錨點表示，提供約 5 倍的存儲減少，相較於 Vanilla 3DGS，且沒有每場景的閾值。位寬策略是主導的速率失真杠杆：統一的 6 位上限在視角依賴場景中成本高達 -6.74 dB，相較於我們的異質分配，與我們開發的信息理論反向水填充分析相匹配。GETA-3DGS 與現有編解碼器互補：熵編碼（HAC++、CompGS）是下游的，因此兩者可以組合使用。
2605.02086.author: Baobing Zhang et.al.
